Brooklyn Camorra.description: A loose grouping of early-20th-century organized crime gangs formed among Italian immigrants from Naples and Campania in Brooklyn, New York City. Active from 1885 to 1918, they engaged in extortion, gambling, and racketeering. Their links to the Italian Camorra were weak to non-existent.

descriptions: The Brooklyn Camorra was absorbed into the Italian-American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Following the downfall of the New York Camorra, Neapolitan or Campanian organized crime groups in New York were absorbed into or merged with the newly dominant Sicilian Mafia groups in New York, creating the modern Italian-American Mafia

descriptions: D'Aquila absorbed the remnants of the Brooklyn Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: absorbing what was left of the Neapolitan Camorra of Brooklyn

descriptions: Rival organization listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: New York Camorra

descriptions: Salvatore D'Aquila, boss of what became the Gambino family, took over the Brooklyn Camorra in 1916.
edges.evidence_quote: 1910–1928 – Salvatore "Toto" D'Aquila – took over the Brooklyn Camorra in 1916 and merged with Al Mineo's gang forming the largest family in New York.

descriptions: The Bontade family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.

descriptions: Motisi clan had rivalry with Stefano Bontade and his clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Inzerillo Mafia clan , Stefano Bontade

descriptions: The Mancuso were investigated alongside the Stillitano in operation Tirreno, suggesting a relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: operazione 'Tirreno' nei confronti dei Piromalli, dei Molè e degli Stillitano

edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Drosi di Rizziconi, tra i Maisano e gli Stillitano.

descriptions: The two clans were in a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Di Paolo-Carfora ed i Crimaldi-Tortora

descriptions: The two clans are in a long-standing conflict, resulting in the murder of Ricucci.
edges.evidence_quote: Pasquale Ricucci, 45 anni, ritenuto dagli investigatori al vertice del clan Lombardi-Ricucci-La Torre, erede dei Romito, da sempre in lotta con il clan dei 'montanari' Li Bergolis-Miucci, è stato ucciso in un agguato compiuto con colpi di fucile sotto la sua abitazione in via San Pietro, nella frazione 'Macchia' di Monte Sant'Angelo.

descriptions: Brother Speed allegedly associated with Outsiders to acquire precursor chemicals for methamphetamine.
edges.evidence_quote: The Brother Speed has allegedly associated with the Hells Angels, the Outsiders, and the Devils Breed in order to acquire precursor chemicals used in the manufacture of methamphetamine.

descriptions: Brass Knuckles MC is a Bandidos support club.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 2021, charges against Decarlo, Heredia and Robert Farrell Grant III, sergeant-at-arms of the Bandidos support club Brass Knuckles MC, were dismissed by the El Paso County District Attorney's Office.

Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia.description: Colombian far-right paramilitary, drug trafficking, and counterinsurgent organization active from 1997 to 2006. Formed on April 18, 1997, as a coalition of paramilitary groups to combat leftist guerrillas such as FARC-EP and ELN. Responsible for numerous human rights abuses, including massacres, forced displacement, and assassinations. Designated as a terrorist organization by Colombia, the EU, and the US until 2014. Demobilized in 2006, with some members later forming successor criminal groups.

Los Ántrax.description: Los Ántrax is a large enforcer unit and hit squad for the Sinaloa Cartel, based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, Mexico. Led by figures such as José Rodrigo Aréchiga Gamboa and Jesús Victoriano, the group is responsible for numerous homicides and provides armed security for drug lord Ismael El Mayo Zambada. It is considered the Sinaloa Cartel's largest and deadliest enforcer unit.

No Limit Soldiers.description: Organized crime group and street gang from Curaçao, originating in the impoverished neighborhood of Koraal Specht in Willemstad. Involved in high-intensity drug trafficking into the Netherlands, contract killings, and other criminal activities. Allegedly connected to the assassination of politician Helmin Wiels. Allies include Colombian cartels and Dutch Penose.

descriptions: No Limit Soldiers member acted as representative for 'Ndrangheta in Brazil.
edges.evidence_quote: a criminal network that included Chendal Wilfrido Rosa, a member of the No Limit Soldiers who was acting as a representative for 'Ndrangheta in Brazil.

descriptions: No Limit Soldiers does business with Colombian crime groups for contract killings.
edges.evidence_quote: Colombian crime groups as well as criminal organizations from the Dutch Penose are known for doing business with the Afro-Curaçaoan gang in the committing of contract killings.

descriptions: No Limit Soldiers does business with Dutch Penose for contract killings.

Gustin Gang.description: Irish-American gang active in Boston during the Prohibition era, formed in the mid-1910s by Frank Wallace and his brother Steve. They engaged in hijacking, armed robbery, and bootlegging, using false badges to steal alcohol from rivals. The gang dominated South Boston's underworld until internal conflict and Italian-American rivals led to its decline.

descriptions: Italian-American gangsters ambushed and killed Gustin Gang leaders during a sit-down.
edges.evidence_quote: On 22 December 1931, after several trucks had been hijacked by members of the Gustins, Frank Wallace and his lieutenant Bernard "Dodo" Walsh were killed in an ambush, after agreeing to a sit-down with Italian-American gangsters , Joe Lombardo (Lombardi) of the North End , at the C.K. Importing Company, 317 Hanover St.

descriptions: Patriarca predecessor Buccola's underboss killed the Gustin Gang boss.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 1931, Lombardo arranged the murder of Frank Wallace, the boss of South Boston's Irish Gustin Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang , Gustin Gang , Winter Hill Gang , and various other gangs in New England

Polish-American organized crime.description: Polish-American organized crime refers to criminal groups composed of Polish immigrants and Polish Americans operating in the United States since the 1920s. Active in cities like Chicago, New York, Philadelphia, and others, they have been involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, loansharking, and other crimes. The Philadelphia-based Kielbasa Posse and the Greenpoint Crew in New York are notable factions. The groups have had alliances with the Chicago Outfit and rivalries with Irish and Russian mobs.

descriptions: Kielbasa Posse is listed as a rival of the K&A Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Various local street gangs , Kielbasa Posse

descriptions: The Polish Mob has a rivalry with the Russian mafia.

Warlocks Motorcycle Club.description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1967 in Florida by ex-US Navy servicemen. Known as a 'One Percenter' club with chapters in the US, Canada, England, and Germany. The mother chapter remains in Orlando, Florida. Founder Tom 'Grub' Freeland died in 2019.

descriptions: The Warlocks kidnapped a Breed chapter president in retaliation for assaults by Breed members.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 1988, members of the Warlocks kidnapped the Breed's Bucks County chapter president Craig "Coyote" Gudkneckt in retaliation for several Warlocks being assaulted by Breed members

descriptions: Infobox lists Warlocks as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels, Pagans, Warlocks

Los Pepes.description: Paramilitary group formed in 1992 by enemies of Pablo Escobar, based in Antioquia, Colombia. Financed by the Cali Cartel and led by the Castaño brothers, they waged a small-scale war against the Medellín Cartel until Escobar's death in 1993. The group had ties to Colombian National Police and later evolved into the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Bloque Metro's leader joined PEPES, which targeted the Medellín cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: García se sumó junto con los Castaño al movimiento denominado « Perseguidos por Pablo Escobar » o los PEPES

descriptions: Cali Cartel helped form Los Pepes to target Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: helped form the vigilante group "Los Pepes"

descriptions: Cali Cartel financed Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was largely financed by the Cali Cartel and led by brothers Carlos and Fidel Castaño whom were right-wing paramilitary leading commanders that were actually once part of the Medellín Cartel.

descriptions: Cali Cartel collaborated with MAS and Medellín dissidents to form Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: the Calí Cartel, dissidents within the Medellín Cartel, and the MAS worked together to create a new paramilitary organization known as Perseguidos Por Pablo Escobar

descriptions: The Cartel de Cali funded Los Pepes to fight Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: el Cartel de Cali abrió su ataque inyectando de efectivo los bolsillos de « Los Pepes »

descriptions: Cali Cartel leader Herrera was a founding member of Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: Herrera is believed to have been a founding member of Los Pepes

descriptions: Cali Cartel founded Los Pepes to target Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Cali Cartel grew in strength, eventually founding Los Pepes

edges.evidence_quote: government crackdowns on the Choneros led to the emergence of violent splinter factions, including Los Pepes, a coalition of eight smaller gangs.

descriptions: Miembros de Los Pepes luego formaron las AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Otros se consolidarían como líderes de las autodefensas, que se agruparían años después bajo el nombre de AUC

descriptions: The River Gang was a predecessor group that later evolved into the Detroit Partnership.

descriptions: The East Side Gang and the River Gang shared bootlegging rackets and other underworld activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Milazzo's Mafia group and the Eastside Gang maintained alliances with other mob factions, including the River Gang, led by brothers Thomas and Peter Licavoli who shared the local bootlegging rackets and various underworld activities with each other.

descriptions: The River Gang was formed by former Egan's Rats members, indicating a successor relationship.

Motisi Mafia clan.description: Historical Sicilian Mafia clan from the Pagliarelli area in Palermo, founded in 1936. One of the oldest active Mafia clans in Palermo, it has been involved in the Sicilian Mafia Commission and allied with the Corleonesi. Members have been convicted for murders of anti-mafia judges and rival clan members. Current boss Giovanni Motisi has been a fugitive since 1993.

Cártel Independiente de Sonora.description: Mexican criminal federation dedicated to drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and other crimes. Originally formed as Gente Nueva, an elite armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel created to combat the Juárez Cartel in northwestern Mexico. In 2023, it split from the Sinaloa Cartel due to internal disputes with Los Chapitos and became independent. Based in Sonora, it operates in Chihuahua, Sonora, and Sinaloa.
